title: Tamil Traditional Service-Promotion Large-Format Banner Set — Print-Prep & Source-File Production (6 sizes)
one_line_ask: Take my supplied Tamil banner master files plus hero photography and brand logo, and produce the complete print-ready production package — graded hero image with print bleed, a clean vector logo, a traditional halftone texture motif, and CMYK 150-dpi PDF/JPEG exports of all six large-format banners — with editable source files alongside.
full_brief:
I run a traditional Tamil service business and I'm rolling out a promotion across six printed banners (design already laid out by my designer): 10x10 ft (3 pieces), 10x20 ft (2 pieces), 10x6 ft (1 piece). All copy is in Tamil and the look must stay clearly traditional — warm, festive, gold/maroon tones, eye-catching from a distance. I will supply you: (1) my authored Illustrator master files for the banners with all Tamil typography already set correctly in licensed Tamil fonts (do NOT re-typeset or translate — typography is locked), (2) one wide hero photograph of my premises/service scene that needs heavy correction and a warm traditional grade, (3) my brand logo as a flat raster file that I need cleaned up into crisp vector for large print, and (4) a low-res PDF proof of last year's banner that I want converted back into an editable layout source.

What I need from you, all on the Adobe production side:
- Fix and grade the hero photo: straighten it, auto-correct tone, warm it up, push the gold/maroon vibrance, pull back blown highlights, then isolate the main service element so its colour stays controlled.
- The hero photo prints right to the edge, so I need 1 inch of real bleed around it for the largest 10x20 ft layout — since the source is an ultra-wide capture with room to spare, reframe and resize it to the bleed-inclusive target dimensions so the existing image content fills all the way into the 1-inch bleed margin (crop to fit, don't just pad).
- Turn my flat logo into a clean scalable vector so it stays sharp at 10-foot height.
- Build me a traditional half-tone / screen-print texture motif derived from the graded hero so it reads as a printed-poster aesthetic up close.
- Export each of the six authored banner masters as a flattened high-res raster and as a print PDF — CMYK intent, 150 dpi minimum at full scale, 1 inch bleed all around.
- Convert my old PDF proof into an editable InDesign source so I can reuse last year's layout.
- Hand back a single packaged folder with every final and source file organised.

Acceptance: traditional consistent look across all sizes, no damage to the locked Tamil typography, and print-ready files meeting CMYK / 150 dpi / 1-inch-bleed specs. One round of minor revisions expected after I review the first draft.

inputs.name: banner_hero_photo.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal wide horizontal photograph of a traditional South Indian service storefront / festive premises scene at golden hour: warm sunlight, marigold garlands, brass lamps, a draped maroon-and-gold cloth backdrop, a small decorated counter or seva table in the foreground. On the wall behind the counter hangs the business signboard carrying the SAME brand as brand_logo_flat.png: the circular maroon-and-gold crest (a brass oil-lamp / kuthuvilakku in the centre, the Tamil-script business name 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' curved along the top arc, a small lotus underline) shown together with the Tamil business name 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை'. That Tamil name is the ONLY brand name anywhere in the frame — no other business name, no English or romanised wordmark on the board. Slightly tilted handheld framing (visibly off-level horizon by ~3 degrees), mildly overexposed sky in the top-right corner, real documentary look — natural skin pores on a person partially in frame, individual marigold petals, dust motes in the light, no CGI gloss. 3:1 ultra-wide aspect, high resolution.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately tilted horizon and one blown-highlight region so straighten / highlight-recovery / auto-tone steps have real work to do. Warm festive palette gives the gold/maroon grade something genuine to push. Must look like a real phone/DSLR capture, not a render. Signboard MUST carry the ONE canonical brand — the same circular kuthuvilakku crest and the Tamil name 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' as on brand_logo_flat.png — so the hero photo and the logo agree on a single brand identity (name, mark, and maroon/gold palette). No 'SRI LAKSHMI SEVA NILAYAM' or any other name.

inputs.name: brand_logo_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, slightly low-resolution raster brand logo for a traditional Tamil service business: a circular emblem with a stylised oil-lamp (kuthuvilakku) motif, the Tamil-script business name 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' curved along the top arc, and a small lotus underline, in solid maroon and gold on a plain white background. 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' is the one and only brand name — no English or romanised wordmark. Crisp solid color fills, no gradients, no photographic texture — the kind of logo that would vectorize cleanly. Render at modest resolution so visible pixel/edge stair-stepping is present.
inputs.realism_notes: Solid-fill, limited-palette emblem is the correct candidate for image_vectorize (raster->clean SVG). Mild aliasing makes the vectorize step a genuine cleanup, not a no-op. Tamil glyphs are baked into the raster (we never re-typeset). This crest — circular kuthuvilakku emblem + Tamil name 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' + lotus underline in maroon/gold — is the CANONICAL master mark; every other brand-carrying asset (e.g. the hero signboard, the banner masters, last year's proof) must show this same name, mark and palette.

inputs.name: banner_master_10x20.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: Author a genuine Adobe Illustrator master document (.ai) for a 10ft x 20ft large-format banner in a traditional Tamil festive style: full Tamil promotional headline and body copy already set in a licensed Tamil OpenType font (real text frames, NOT outlined), maroon/gold ornamental border, placeholder frames for a hero photograph and a circular logo. Document set to CMYK, 1-inch bleed defined on all sides, 150 dpi target at full scale. This is the largest of the six authored banner masters.
inputs.realism_notes: AUTHORED INPUT (not connector-produced). Layout composition and Tamil text-setting are out of connector scope, so the client supplies the real .ai master. document_render_vector only EXPORTS this genuine .ai — it does not compose it. The five other size masters are sibling files of the same family.

inputs.name: banner_masters_remaining.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: Author the remaining five genuine Illustrator banner master documents matching banner_master_10x20.ai's traditional Tamil style and locked typography: three at 10ft x 10ft and two more at 10ft x 6ft / 10ft x 20ft per the size breakdown, each CMYK with 1-inch bleed and 150 dpi target, Tamil copy already set in licensed fonts, consistent maroon/gold ornamental treatment.
inputs.realism_notes: AUTHORED INPUT bundle representing the rest of the six-banner family (sizes: 10x10 x3, 10x20 x2, 10x6 x1). Supplied by client so the connector can EXPORT them. Keeps the chain honest — no from-scratch layout by the agent.

inputs.name: last_year_proof.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: A single-page low-resolution PDF proof of last year's traditional Tamil promotional banner for the SAME client as brand_logo_flat.png — the traditional Tamil service business 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' (Parambariya Sevai). Maroon/gold ornamental frame, a Tamil headline, a photo block, and the client's circular kuthuvilakku crest logo showing the Tamil name 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை', laid out in landscape. Use only this one brand name — no other client or business name. Make it a flattened export-style PDF (no live editable layers) so converting it back into an editable layout is a meaningful task.
inputs.realism_notes: Feeds document_convert_pdf (PDF -> genuine editable .indd). A flattened proof is exactly the realistic input for that PDF->.indd conversion the client requested for reuse. Client/brand is the canonical 'பாரம்பரிய சேவை' with the circular kuthuvilakku crest — same single identity as every other brand-carrying asset in this task.

outputs.name: hero_graded_bleed.png
outputs.spec: Corrected + warm-traditional-graded hero photograph (straightened, auto-toned, highlights recovered, gold/maroon vibrance pushed, controlled subject color) with 1 inch of real reframe-cropped bleed on all sides for the 10x20 ft layout. High-res PNG, sRGB working copy for placement into the authored master.

outputs.name: brand_logo_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Clean scalable SVG vectorization of the flat brand logo, sharp at 10-foot print height, solid maroon/gold fills, Tamil emblem preserved.

outputs.name: halftone_texture_motif.png
outputs.spec: Traditional screen-print / halftone texture motif derived from the graded hero, for the close-up printed-poster aesthetic. High-res PNG.

outputs.name: banner_10x20_print.pdf + .png
outputs.spec: Print export of the authored 10x20 ft master: CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched. (CMYK intent applies to the PDF; the PNG is an RGB companion proof.)

outputs.name: banner_10x10_a_print.pdf + .png
outputs.spec: Print export of the first authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_a): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.

outputs.name: banner_10x10_b_print.pdf + .png
outputs.spec: Print export of the second authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_b): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.

outputs.name: banner_10x10_c_print.pdf + .png
outputs.spec: Print export of the third authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_c): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.

outputs.name: banner_10x20_b_print.pdf + .png
outputs.spec: Print export of the second authored 10x20 ft master (banner_10x20_b): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.

outputs.name: banner_10x6_print.pdf + .png
outputs.spec: Print export of the authored 10x6 ft master: CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.

outputs.name: banner_set_delivery_folder
outputs.spec: Organised CC delivery folder (populated via connector-side asset copy, not a zip archive) containing the print PDF + high-res PNG exports for all six authored banner masters (3x 10x10, 2x 10x20, 1x 10x6), the editable .indd converted from last year's proof, the vector logo, the graded hero, and the halftone motif — every final and source file organised in one deliverable.

connector_workflow.note: Client supplies banner_hero_photo.jpg. Initialize the upload session for the hero photograph into CC storage. First asset load — no prior-step input.
Finalize the hero upload and obtain the stored asset URL that the editing chain will consume.
Correct the ~3-degree tilted horizon in the hero capture.
Baseline tonal correction on the straightened image.
Pull back the blown-out sky/highlight region carried from the prior step.
Warm the white balance toward the traditional festive gold palette.
Push gold/maroon richness on the warmed image for distance legibility.
Mask the main service element (e.g. the brass lamp / seva counter) on the graded image to control its colour.
Apply masked single-colour saturation so the focal element's colour stays controlled — selective edit via the step-8 mask.

GEN-FREE bleed: the source is a 3:1 ultra-wide capture with room to spare, so reframe and resize the graded hero to the bleed-inclusive target dimensions for the 10x20 ft layout — the existing image content is cropped/scaled to fill all the way into the 1-inch print bleed on every side (no invented pixels, no padding). Deliverable #1.
Client supplies brand_logo_flat.png. Initialize upload of the flat raster logo. Second asset load.
Finalize logo upload, obtain stored URL for vectorization.
Vectorize the flat solid-fill logo into a clean SVG, sharp at 10-foot height. Deliverable #2.
Derive a traditional screen-print halftone texture motif from the graded+bleed hero. Deliverable #3 (consumes step-10 output).
Client supplies authored banner_master_10x20.ai (the largest single master). Initialize upload of the genuine .ai master that will be EXPORTED (not composed).
Finalize the authored 10x20 .ai master upload, obtain its stored URL.

EXPORT the genuine authored 10x20 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Deliverable #4 (first of six masters).

Client supplies the banner_masters_remaining.ai bundle (the other five authored masters: 3x 10x10, 1x 10x20, 1x 10x6). Initialize upload of the genuine sibling masters that will be EXPORTED (not composed).
Finalize the remaining-masters upload, obtain the stored URL(s) for the five sibling .ai masters.
EXPORT the first authored 10x10 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Second of six masters.
EXPORT the second authored 10x10 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Third of six masters.
EXPORT the third authored 10x10 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Fourth of six masters.
EXPORT the second authored 10x20 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Fifth of six masters.
EXPORT the authored 10x6 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Sixth of six masters — all six now rendered.
Client supplies last_year_proof.pdf. Initialize upload of the flattened proof PDF for back-conversion.
Convert the supplied proof PDF into a genuine editable .indd source so the client can reuse last year's layout.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: banner_10x20_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x10_a_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x10_b_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x10_c_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x20_b_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x6_print.pdf + .png, last_year_editable.indd
connector_workflow.note: Create the organised delivery folder structure for finals + sources.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: delivery_folder, hero_graded_bleed.png, brand_logo_vector.svg, halftone_texture_motif.png, banner_10x20_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x10_a_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x10_b_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x10_c_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x20_b_print.pdf + .png, banner_10x6_print.pdf + .png, last_year_editable.indd
connector_workflow.note: Copy every produced final + source (all six banner exports, the hero bleed, logo SVG, halftone, and editable indd) into the delivery folder for the single packaged hand-off. Final deliverable — the packaging is connector-side asset_copy into an organised CC folder (not a zip archive), so no separate [L] step needed.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Hero pipeline: upload(1->2) -> auto_straighten(3) -> auto_tone(4) -> highlights(5) -> color_temp(6) -> vibrance(7), each consuming the prior frame; select_by_prompt(8) builds a mask FROM hero_graded, which feeds the masked single_color_saturation(9) [image + mask], whose output is reframe-cropped for bleed(10 -> hero_graded_bleed.png). The halftone motif(14) consumes step-10's bleed output. Logo branch: upload(11->12) -> vectorize(13). Authored-master branch: the 10x20 master uploads(15->16) -> render_vector EXPORT(17), then the remaining-five-master bundle uploads(18->19) and each sibling is EXPORTED by its own render_vector call (20 -> 10x10_a, 21 -> 10x10_b, 22 -> 10x10_c, 23 -> 10x20_b, 24 -> 10x6), so all six authored masters are individually rendered to CMYK PDF + PNG. Proof branch: upload(25) -> convert_pdf(26). Packaging: create_folders(27) consumes all six exports + indd, then copy_assets(28) consumes EVERY produced deliverable (hero bleed, logo SVG, halftone, all six banner exports, editable indd) into one organised delivery folder. The final banner package emerges only from the assembled chain.
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real Freelancer.com brief: six banners in Tamil, traditional style; size breakdown 10x10 ft (3), 10x20 ft (2), 10x6 ft (1); copy supplied in Tamil; eye-catching from a distance; editable source files (AI/PSD) alongside final PDFs/JPEGs; CMYK, 150 dpi minimum at full scale, 1 inch bleed all around; acceptance on accurate Tamil typography with no spelling errors, consistent traditional look, print-ready files; one round of minor revisions. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: the original is a from-scratch design + Tamil-typesetting + layout-composition job, which is OUT of connector scope (layout composition and text-from-scratch are [X]/not-headless). So the runnable version reframes the connector's role to the genuine print-PRODUCTION half: the client supplies the authored .ai banner masters (Tamil already typeset in licensed fonts — never re-typeset, protecting the 'no spelling errors' acceptance criterion), a hero photo, a flat logo, and an old PDF proof; the connector does the real repeatable production — photo correction + traditional warm grade, masked colour control, real bleed via reframe-crop, logo vectorization for large-format sharpness, a halftone print-texture motif, EXPORT of the authored masters to CMYK/150-dpi/bleed PDFs+PNGs, PDF->.indd back-conversion for reuse, and packaging. This is faithful to the brief's stated deliverables (print-ready PDFs/JPEGs + editable sources, CMYK/150dpi/bleed) without inventing an unrelated task; the only honest shift is WHO authors the locked Tamil layout (client) vs WHO produces the print-ready assets and exports (the connector).
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (28 connector calls) across three Adobe groups (image_*, document_*, asset_*) with 16 distinct Adobe tools — well past the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar. It is a true dependency chain: an 8-step hero grade that must be applied in the correct order (straighten before tone, highlights before warming, mask before masked-saturation, bleed last), a branched logo-vectorize path, an authored-master export path that renders all six masters individually, a PDF->.indd back-conversion, and a final packaging fan-in that consumes every deliverable. The hard reasoning is feasibility discipline: recognizing that Tamil typesetting and layout composition are out of scope so the masters must be AUTHORED INPUTS that document_render_vector only EXPORTS; that bleed must be an honest reframe-crop (image_crop_and_resize reframes/resizes the existing ultra-wide source to the bleed-inclusive target ratio, accepting that edges are cropped — no padding, no generative fill, no canvas extension); that the focal colour control needs a select_by_prompt mask feeding a masked adjustment; and that every one of the six masters needs its own EXPORT call rather than a single asserted 'repeat'. All print-spec acceptance criteria (CMYK PDF, 150 dpi at full scale, 1-inch bleed, untouched Tamil type) are honoured by the export/authoring split. Every step is execution-mode tagged [C] and headless-confirmed — no [W]/[A]/[T]/[L] crutches.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned/generative capability remains. The bleed step (n=10) uses image_crop_and_resize, which reframes/resizes the existing ultra-wide source to the bleed-inclusive target ratio without inventing pixels — an honest crop, not padding, not canvas extension, and not generative fill. No generative-expand/outpaint capability appears anywhere. No upscale, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art, no object-removal-with-reconstruction anywhere in the chain. Remaining tools are all deterministic photo edits, vectorize (raster->SVG cleanup), halftone effect, authored-master EXPORT, PDF->INDD conversion, and asset management — none generative.
reverify.notes: Chaining preserved: hero_subject_controlled -> image_crop_and_resize -> hero_graded_bleed.png, still consumed by image_apply_halftone (n=14) and the packaging fan-in (n=28). Outputs completeness fixed: all six authored masters are now individually rendered — the 10x20 (n=17) plus the five siblings from banner_masters_remaining.ai (n=20 10x10_a, n=21 10x10_b, n=22 10x10_c, n=23 10x20_b, n=24 10x6) — each to a CMYK PDF + sRGB PNG, matching the brief's 'all six' promise and the six PDF/PNG output entries. Tool count is now 28 (five added render_vector EXPORT calls plus a second master-upload init/finalize pair); distinct_adobe_tools stays 16 (all added tools already present). CMYK intent is attached only to the PDF exports; the PNG companions are RGB/sRGB. The final package is an organised CC delivery folder populated via asset_copy_assets (renamed banner_set_delivery_folder), not a zip archive.
